objective: Make every durable global pause an irreversible fence for attempts claimed before that pause, while allowing only newly claimed attempts after a verified resume to progress.
executable_blocker: The canonical ledger currently accepts checkpoint and completion writes from a pre-pause attempt during active pause and after resume and reopen.

required: compare the winning claim event sequence with the latest durable pause event sequence inside the existing immediate transaction
deny checkpoint and completion when a pause sequence is newer than the attempt claim sequence
persist the fence across resume process restart and ledger reopen
allow an attempt claimed after verified resume to use normal checkpoint and completion semantics
all denials append zero events and preserve the existing hash chain
forbidden: new event type schema migration trigger index table or second ledger
in-memory pause generation active-pause-only check or resume revival
admission kernel control IPC domain or budget behavior change

acceptance_commands: exact write-set schema dependency skip privacy network and live-authority scan
rollback: Agent 0 discards the single worker commit and deletes the isolated branch/worktree. The canonical ledger schema and any real runtime database remain unchanged.
